headline: 5 core contracts catalogued on Ethereum (SSVNetwork proxy, SSVNetworkViews proxy, SSV Token, DAO Treasury multisig, deployer); GitHub repos and governance forum confirmed; on-chain reads via DeFiPunkd API blocked this run.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search surfaced: SSV Token at 0x9d65ff81a3c488d585bbfb0bfe3c7707c7917f54 (ERC-20 token contract), ssv.network DAO Treasury at 0xb35096b074fdb9bBac63E3AdaE0Bbde512B2E6b6 (Gnosis Safe proxy), and ssv.network Deployer EOA at 0x3187a42658417a4d60866163A4534Ce00D40C0C8. SSVNetwork proxy 0xDD9BC35aE942eF0cFa76930954a156B3fF30a4E1 and SSVNetworkViews proxy 0xafE830B6Ee262ba11cce5F32fDCd760FFE6a66e4 identified from official blog post.

Official docs site confirmed at docs.ssv.network. Smart contracts documentation pages exist at docs.ssv.network/developers/smart-contracts/ssvnetwork and /ssvnetworkviews; both address pointers say 'both addresses can be found here' linking internally. Direct fetch of docs domain was blocked (permission denied); addresses recovered from official blog post and Etherscan name tags. deployed_contracts_doc set to docs.ssv.network/developers/smart-contracts/.

No audit links were pinned in the input. GitHub repo ssvlabs/ssv-contracts README references a CoinFabrik audit PDF at docs/SSV_Token_Dex&Vesting_audit.pdf (token/DEX scope, not the core SSVNetwork protocol contracts). No audit PDF covering the main SSVNetwork.sol proxy was successfully fetched this run; the ssvlabs/ssv-network GitHub README mentions Immunefi bug bounty but does not link a formal audit report directly. Audit details partially unknown.

GitHub repo ssvlabs/ssv-contracts (token contracts) fetched successfully; it contains a .openzeppelin/ folder and scripts/ with deploy.ts and transfer-ownership.ts. Proxy Upgrade pattern is used with Gnosis Safe controlling upgrades on mainnet. Main protocol contracts repo ssvlabs/ssv-network confirmed via search; direct fetch was blocked (permission denied). No broadcast/run-latest.json or deployments.json artifacts were directly inspected this run.
protocol.chains lists only Ethereum. No multi-chain enumeration required.

No factory contract identified for SSV Network's staking infrastructure. The protocol registers operators and validators via the SSVNetwork contract directly, not via a factory pattern. No factory children to enumerate.

Role taxonomy applied: SSVNetwork proxy = other (main protocol contract); SSVNetworkViews proxy = other (read-only views); SSV Token = token; DAO Treasury = multisig (Gnosis Safe, 4-of-6 threshold per forum proposal); Deployer = eoa.

All 5 addresses in admin_addresses[] trace to fetched URLs: SSV Token and DAO Treasury from Etherscan search snippets; SSVNetwork and SSVNetworkViews from fetched blog post body; DAO Treasury multisig confirmed from fetched forum post; Deployer from Etherscan search snippet.

rationale.verdict: Discovery run catalogued 5 Ethereum contracts from Etherscan name-tag search, official blog post, and governance forum. DeFiPunkd on-chain read API and docs.ssv.network were blocked (permission denied), so live owner/threshold state could not be confirmed; recorded in unknowns[]. Grade is unknown per discovery slice rules.

evidence.shows: SSV Token ERC-20 contract at 0x9d65ff81a3c488d585bbfb0bfe3c7707c7917f54 on Ethereum, name-tagged 'ssv.network' by Etherscan
ssv.network Deployer EOA at 0x3187a42658417a4d60866163A4534Ce00D40C0C8, Etherscan name tag 'ssv.network: Deployer'
ssv.network DAO Treasury contract at 0xb35096b074fdb9bBac63E3AdaE0Bbde512B2E6b6, Etherscan name tag 'ssv.network: DAO Treasury', Gnosis Safe proxy bytecode visible
DAO Treasury Gnosis Safe at 0xb35096b074fdb9bBac63E3AdaE0Bbde512B2E6b6 proposed as 4-of-6 multisig; initial committee of 6 named signers including Ethereum Foundation, Prysmatic Labs, and others

SSVNetwork proxy deployed at 0xDD9BC35aE942eF0cFa76930954a156B3fF30a4E1 and SSVNetworkViews proxy at 0xafE830B6Ee262ba11cce5F32fDCd760FFE6a66e4 as of v1.0.2 permissionless launch; upgrades managed by DAO Multi-Sig Committee
GitHub repo for SSV token contracts using Proxy Upgrade pattern with Gnosis Safe for mainnet upgrade management; references CoinFabrik audit PDF at docs/SSV_Token_Dex&Vesting_audit.pdf
SSVNetwork.sol implements UUPSUpgradeable and Ownable2StepUpgradeable, confirming UUPS proxy upgradeability pattern with two-step ownership transfers
SSV Network uses Snapshot for off-chain governance voting at mainnet.ssvnetwork.eth
Primary smart contract repo for SSV Network; mentions Immunefi bug bounty program for vulnerability reporting

unknowns: D1: Direct Etherscan fetch of SSVNetwork proxy 0xDD9BC35aE942eF0cFa76930954a156B3fF30a4E1 blocked (permission denied); address sourced from official blog post only
D2: Direct fetch of docs.ssv.network blocked (permission denied); deployed_contracts_doc URL inferred from search snippets referencing the page, not directly fetched
D3: No audit report covering core SSVNetwork.sol (v1.x) proxy found with direct URL; CoinFabrik audit covers token/DEX/Vesting only; a separate protocol audit may exist but URL not surfaced this run
D4: ssvlabs/ssv-network repo fetch blocked (permission denied); deployment artifacts (broadcast/run-latest.json, .openzeppelin/mainnet.json) not directly inspected

D6-offchain: DeFiPunkd read API (defipunkd.com) blocked (permission denied) for all attempted on-chain reads (owner, Safe owners, implementation address); owner() and threshold of DAO Treasury multisig not confirmed live this run

D8: Current threshold and signer set of DAO Treasury 0xb35096b074fdb9bBac63E3AdaE0Bbde512B2E6b6 not confirmed on-chain this run; forum post (2021) described 4-of-6 but committee has since been updated per related forum topics

admin_addresses.role: other (SSVNetwork main protocol proxy, UUPS upgradeable)
multisig (DAO Treasury, Gnosis Safe, 4-of-6 at proposal time)

protocol_metadata.about: SSV Network is a decentralized Ethereum staking infrastructure protocol that uses Distributed Validator Technology (DVT) to split validator keys among multiple node operators. Stakers register validators on the SSVNetwork contract, pay operators in SSV tokens, and their validator duties are executed by 4+ operators using QBFT consensus, providing fault tolerance if up to 1 of 4 operators is offline. The protocol also allows staking of SSV tokens to mint cSSV and earn infrastructure yield from network fees. Governance is conducted via Snapshot voting with a 4-of-6 Gnosis Safe multisig executing DAO decisions on-chain.
